data.description: Earnings reports of various companies are set to be released before the bell, with X number of companies providing updates on their financial performance. Additionally, Y number of companies are scheduled to report after the market closes. Further details on these earnings announcements can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Greenbrier's Q1 earnings beat estimates: $1.14 per share vs 87 cents; sales $706.1M vs $626.3M. Stock down 0.8% to $52.90.
data.description: This morning on Friday, U.S. stock futures are trading slightly lower. One of the stocks catching investor attention today is Greenbrier Companies Inc (NYSE: GBX), which reported strong first-quarter earnings after the closing bell on Thursday. The company's earnings were $1.14 per share, surpassing market predictions of 87 cents per share. Quarterly sales totaled $706.100 million, higher than the expected $626.297 million according to Benzinga Pro. Following this news, Greenbrier shares experienced a 0.8% decrease, settling at $52.90. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Linde to announce Q4 2025 earnings on Feb 5, 2026, at 06:00 EST. Conference call at 09:00 EST. US: 1 888 770 7292, UK: 0800 358 0970.
data.description: Woking, England - Linde (Nasdaq: LIN) is set to announce its financial results for the fourth quarter of 2025 at 06:00 EST/midday CET on Thursday, February 5, 2026. The company will conduct a conference call at 09:00 EST/15:00 CET on the same day, which will be in listen-only mode and open to the public and media. For those interested, the US Toll-Free Dial-In Number is 1 888 770 7292, and the UK Toll-Free Dial-In Number is 0800 358 0970, with the access code being 6877110. The live webcast (listen-only) can be accessed at https://www.linde.com/in.

data.title: Oklo and Meta partner for 1.2 GW nuclear energy project in Ohio to support Meta data centers with prepay for power.
data.description: In Pike County, Ohio, Oklo Inc. (NYSE: OKLO), an advanced nuclear technology company, has entered into a partnership with Meta Platforms, Inc. (Nasdaq: META). This agreement aims to support Oklo's development of a 1.2 GW power campus to cater to Meta's data centers in the area. Through this deal, Meta will have the option to prepay for power and offer financial backing to enhance the project's certainty for Oklo's Aurora powerhouse deployment. The funding provided by Meta will be allocated towards securing nuclear fuel for the project.

data.title: Hundreds of students to celebrate Utah Fits All Scholarship at Juan Diego Catholic High School in two weeks.
data.description: In Draper, Utah, on January 9, 2026, hundreds of students along with their families from various parts of Utah are set to assemble at the Juan Diego Catholic High School in a fortnight to commemorate the influence of the Utah Fits All Scholarship. This gathering aims to emphasize the positive effects of the scholarship program on the recipients.

data.title: Aurora Therapeutics founded with $16M to develop personalized gene editing for rare diseases at scale.
data.description: A new biotech company, Aurora Therapeutics, has been officially launched in Boston with the goal of revolutionizing personalized gene editing. The company aims to expand from treating just one patient to developing therapies for millions of individuals with rare diseases. Aurora has received a $16 million seed funding from Menlo Ventures to establish the initial platform for addressing rare genetic mutations that were previously unmanageable on a larger scale. Founded by Jennifer Doudna, Ph.D., the company's mission is to make breakthrough treatments accessible to a wider population.

data.title: Tucson School Fair on Jan. 24 at Children's Museum showcasing 36+ public, charter schools to help parents choose.
data.description: In Tucson, Arizona, on January 9, 2026, parents are invited to explore various school options for their children at the upcoming Tucson School Fair on January 24. The event will take place at the Children's Museum of Tucson starting at 10:00 AM. Over 36 public, charter, and private schools will be represented at the fair, providing parents with a comprehensive overview of educational opportunities available in the area.

data.title: Top 20 press releases of 2025 summarized by PR Newswire in New York, detailing major stories that attracted reader interest.
data.description: In a review of the most impactful press releases distributed through PR Newswire in the last year, New York, Jan. 9, 2026 - PR Newswire looks back on the top stories that resonated with audiences in 2025. These releases garnered significant attention, with hundreds of articles, social media shares, and views, highlighting their widespread reach and influence.

data.title: TerraPower & Meta to build 8 Natrium® Advanced Nuclear Plants for US grid, supporting energy demand growth.
data.description: An agreement has been reached to enhance the reliability of the U.S. grid by introducing carbon-free power to meet the rising energy demand. The collaboration between TerraPower, a leading nuclear innovation firm, and Meta will involve the development of a maximum of 8 Natrium reactor 1 units alongside energy storage solutions. This initiative, announced in Bellevue, Washington on January 9, 2026, marks a significant step towards bolstering the energy infrastructure, indicating a keen focus on sustainable and efficient energy production.

data.title: Greater Columbus School Fair on Jan. 24 at Columbus Zoo & Aquarium to showcase local education options for parents.
data.description: Discover the varied educational opportunities for children in Central Ohio at the Greater Columbus K-12 School Fair on Saturday, January 24, 2026. Starting at 11:00 AM at the Columbus Zoo & Aquarium, parents can explore numerous school options for their kids in one convenient location.

data.title: Vistra and Meta sign 20-year PPAs to add 2,600+ MW to grid, supporting nuclear plants in PJM.
data.description: Vistra, a corporation publicly traded under the symbol NYSE: VST, has recently disclosed their arrangement of long-term power purchase agreements (PPAs) spanning a 20-year period. These agreements are designed to bolster Meta's operational capacity while concurrently enhancing the energy production of three nuclear facilities belonging to Vistra within the PJM region. Specifically, these contracts will facilitate the delivery of over 2,600 megawatts of power over the stipulated duration, marking a significant advancement in their energy provision strategy.
